Feature Type: Raw time-series waveforms (per-receiver vertical component)
Feature Parameters: Not specified beyond 12 receivers and dilation/upsampling within network
Task Framing: Multi-task regression: predicts denoised waveform and event score; single MSE loss used for both; events binarized via threshold
Architecture Details: DCNN with three parallel upsampled dilated conv branches (→72 features each), summed → CNN → downsampled to 24 features (12 denoised + 12 phases); GRU branch in parallel + final GRU; Tanh activations; linear output head; Adam optimizer (lr=0.001); ~5000 training iterations
Post-processing: Threshold 0.6 on event output to produce binary picks (time indices)
Key Hyperparameters: Adam lr=0.001; Tanh activations; features 72→24; threshold=0.6; ~5000 iterations; (dilations used, specific rates not listed)
Performance Metrics: Training/validation MSE; qualitative overlays; blind-data scatter and density plots; event-pick hit count
Performance Metrics Results: Blind subset: all 10 true events correctly predicted after thresholding; denoised predictions closely match blind filtered targets (scatter shows strong linearity; density overlap)
Latency / Detection Delay: Not reported; authors note near real-time suitability of trained model
Robustness / Domain Shift: Tested on blind dataset (different receivers/segments) from same field experiment; satisfactory qualitative generalization claimed
Hardware & Compute Cost: Not reported

Attribute/Feature Selection: Typically binary (event vs noise) for detection; multi-class for phase types (P/S/other) or mechanism categories; continuous targets for location/magnitude
Train/Val/Test Split Protocol: Strongly imbalanced (rare events vs abundant noise); addressed with resampling, focal/weighted losses, curated windows
Class Balance: Sliding or triggered windows around picks; typical lengths from ~1–10 s depending on sampling rate; overlapping windows for detection
Windowing: Per-trace standardization, amplitude normalization, band-pass filtering; sometimes instrument response removal
Normalization/Scaling: Band-pass/BP filters, wavelet shrinkage, spectral gating, median filters; stacking/array processing; DSS/beamforming prior to ML in some workflows
Denoising Ops: Raw waveforms; time–frequency (STFT, CWT) images; polarization features; array/beam features; pick-time intervals
Feature Type: Sample rate (e.g., 1–4 kHz typical downhole), window length, hop size, frequency bands; chosen per study/site
Feature Parameters: Classification (event vs noise; phase ID), object detection on time axis, regression (arrival times, location coordinates, magnitude/moment), sequence labeling
Task Framing: Classical ML (SVM, Random Forest, GBDT) on engineered features; Deep Learning: 1D/2D CNNs, RNN/LSTM/GRU, U-Net, ResNet, attention/Transformers (emerging), graph/GCN for array geometry; end-to-end and hybrid physics-informed models
Architecture Details: Weighted/focal loss, oversampling of events, undersampling noise, hard-negative mining, synthetic mixing
Class-Imbalance Handling: Non-maximum suppression on time axis, association across channels/stations, phase linking, location inversion, clustering, quality control thresholds
Post-processing: Learning rate & schedule, batch size, window length/hop, network depth/filters, loss choices (CE, focal, MSE/Huber), regularization (dropout, weight decay)
Key Hyperparameters: Additive noise, time shift/jitter, amplitude scaling, band-drop, time-stretch/compress, mixup between traces, random channel/dropout, simulated events injected into noise
Augmentations: Precision/Recall, F1/Fβ, ROC-AUC/PR-AUC; pick error (ms), MAE/RMSE for regression; location error (m), magnitude error; detection rate at fixed FAR
Performance Metrics: Heterogeneous across datasets and sites; no single universal SOTA due to lack of standardized benchmarks; many studies report large gains vs classic STA/LTA baselines on in-domain data
Performance Metrics Results: Emphasis on near real-time operation; reported end-to-end latencies often sub-second to a few seconds depending on windowing and hardware
Latency / Detection Delay: Varies by pipeline; streaming models with short windows achieve lower delay at potential cost to accuracy; batch association adds delay but improves localization consistency
Latency / Detection Delay Results: Generalization across fields/stages is challenging; transfer learning/domain adaptation recommended; performance can drop significantly off-site without adaptation
Robustness / Domain Shift: Both offline (post-hoc reprocessing) and real-time monitoring deployments are covered
Mode: Classical ML: CPU-friendly; Deep models: GPUs preferred for training and low-latency inference (edge/embedded GPUs or CPU with MKL); compute scales with channels and stations; costs vary by deployment

Attribute/Feature Selection: Entropy‑based discretization of continuous sensor readings to map into discrete states before Markov modeling
Train/Val/Test Split Protocol: NR
Windowing: Spatiotemporal sliding window of size n×W over target and neighbor nodes (W not specified)
Denoising Ops: NR
Feature Type: (i) Temporal: state‑transition probability matrix of target node (first‑order Markov) (ii) Spatial: cross‑state transition probability matrix between target and neighbor nodes
Feature Parameters: 9 discrete states (a–i) via entropy‑based discretization; first‑order Markov assumption; probability matrices row‑normalized
Task Framing: Supervised regression (SVR with RBF kernel) predicting/detecting microseismic energy/abnormality levels
Architecture Details: SVR (RBF kernel) with penalty factor D and kernel parameter γ optimized by improved PSO (adds passive‑aggregation attraction term). PSO uses inertia weight ω∈[0.3,0.9]; coefficients c₁,c₂ (~1.5 typical), c₃ (~1); iteration coefficient β; vmax capped; best empirical settings found: ω=0.9; particle count=45
Post-processing: NR
Key Hyperparameters: SVR: (D, γ, ε). PSO: particle count (tested 15/30/45/60), inertia weight (tested 0.3/0.5/0.7/0.9), acceleration/weight factors (c₁,c₂,c₃), vmax, Nmax, β
Performance Metrics: Missed detection rate (∂), Misjudgment rate (Φ), Sensitivity (ℵ = ξ/ω using amplitude ξ and inertia weight ω), RMSE as PSO fitness, overall detection accuracy
Performance Metrics Results: • Accuracy ≈ 97.3% (from inversion of fitness/ MSE at particle count 45) • Missed‑detection rate kept <3% across 9 operation points (below 6% threshold) • Misjudgment rate kept ≤5% threshold (baseline exceeded at some points) • Sensitivity peaked at ≈55.6 mV when ω=0.9; amplitude up to ≈50 mV • Proposed method correctly captured accident‑time energy surge; baseline lagged
Latency / Detection Delay: Proposed: real‑time (sliding‑window + on‑line feature extraction + SVR). Baseline system showed ~1 min lag during accident sequence; proposed tracked event at the correct time
Latency / Detection Delay Results: Qualitative: accurate at accident time vs. baseline’s delayed high energy one minute later
Robustness / Domain Shift: Examined across four excitation forms and distances (20–90 m); pipeline tapping showed best clarity and attenuation behavior; stability tested at 9 operation points
Mode: Online, real‑time detection in a working face with 9 operation points
Hardware & Compute Cost: NR; simulation tools referenced: LS‑DYNA (nonlinear dynamics), LS‑PrePost 6.0, D3PLOT; future work notes Hadoop big‑data platform and algorithm parallelization

Attribute/Feature Selection: Preprocessing: DC bias & spike removal; per‑sample median subtraction across channels; band‑pass 10–150 Hz; trace normalization; scaling to [0,255] for training
Train/Val/Test Split Protocol: Supervised training with 20% random validation each iteration; 100 epochs with early stopping (patience 10). Test set not explicitly reported.
Windowing: Detection windows overlapped 25%; training crops randomly around event apex; noise windows ≥30 s from any event.
Normalization/Scaling: Trace normalization; scaling to 0–255 for CNN inputs.
Denoising Ops: DC & spike removal; channel‑wise median subtraction; 10–150 Hz band‑pass; visualization normalization.
Feature Type: 2D DAS space–time images (for CNN); 1D traces (for U‑Net).
Feature Parameters: CNN conv kernels 3×3; ReLU; dropout increasing 0.10→0.25; fully‑connected 128; sigmoid output. U‑Net 1D conv kernel size 7; skip connections; softmax output.
Task Framing: Binary classification (event vs noise) and pixel/point‑level segmentation (P/S arrival picking).
Architecture Details: CNN: 5 down‑sampling blocks (conv→ReLU→downsample) + FC(128)→sigmoid; cross‑entropy loss; built in Keras/TensorFlow. U‑Net: 4 down + 4 up stages with skip connections; softmax probabilities for P/S/noise.
Class-Imbalance Handling: Balanced sampling for detection; data augmentation to vary SNR and timing.
Post-processing: Common‑event association across fibers within 1 s; probability threshold 0.3 for P/S picks; hypocenter location via ray tracing + grid search using 1‑D velocity model (sonic logs).
Key Hyperparameters: CNN: batch 32, lr 0.001, epochs up to 100, early‑stop patience 10. U‑Net: batch 100, lr 0.0001, epochs up to 100; pick threshold 0.3.
Augmentations: Inject random noise/jitter into STA/LTA pick times; random cropping around apex; curated background noise windows.
Performance Metrics: Accuracy, Precision, Recall for detection; catalog growth factor vs. STA/LTA; false‑positive rate; qualitative agreement vs. 3C geophones; location coherence with LF‑DAS fracture growth.
Performance Metrics Results: Detection (Case 1): Accuracy 96.62%, Precision 98.73%, Recall 95.51%. Catalog increase: 2.6× (Case 1: 586 vs 224 STA/LTA) and 5.6× (Case 2: 1,459 vs 257). Lower false positives than STA/LTA; DAS catalogs broadly agree in time distribution with geophones but detect fewer very small events.
Latency / Detection Delay: Not explicitly reported; workflow designed for near‑real‑time use post‑training.
Robustness / Domain Shift: Applied to a second field dataset with brief retraining; robust stage‑wise counts; vertical fiber near stage shows performance comparable to geophones in one stage.
Mode: Automated processing with minimal human intervention after training; near‑real‑time monitoring & risk management intended.
Hardware & Compute Cost: Specific hardware not reported; paper notes low computation cost and efficiency after training.

Attribute/Feature Selection: End-to-end learning; no shallow skip-connections (to reduce irrelevant detail); spatial attention to emphasize effective waveforms; ASPP to enlarge receptive field and capture multi-scale features.
Train/Val/Test Split Protocol: Not explicitly specified; validation used for learning-rate scheduling.
Windowing: Time-axis downsampling so number of time samples equals Nz (depth grid) for alignment.
Normalization/Scaling: Batch Normalization in every layer; other input normalization not specified.
Denoising Ops: Robustness via data augmentation (20%–60% Gaussian noise); attention module suppresses noise during feature extraction.
Feature Type: Raw multi-channel microseismic waveforms (2D gathers).
Feature Parameters: ASPP dilation rates: 6, 12, 18; convolution kernels 3×3.
Task Framing: Supervised regression (image-to-image): waveform → 2D source-location probability.
Architecture Details: U‑Net backbone with 6-layer encoder–decoder; spatial attention; ASPP bridge; ReLU activations; Sigmoid output; MSE loss; Adam optimizer.
Post-processing: Argmax of probability image (grid cell with maximum probability) for location readout.
Key Hyperparameters: Initial LR=0.01 (halved on 4-epoch plateau); batch size=64; epochs/iterations: 400 (or 200 when training set multiplied with heavy noise); optimizer=Adam; loss=MSE.
Augmentations: Additive Gaussian noise (20%–60% of max amplitude); velocity-model smoothing with 4×4 and 6×6 operators; multi-source label superposition and normalization.
Performance Metrics: Location error (grid distance / meters), prediction probability at peak; qualitative robustness analyses; compute time.
Performance Metrics Results: Simple model: noiseless predictions nearly coincide with truth; with 20%–40% noise, most errors <10 m; maximum observed ≈11.2 m across 120 tests; multi-source examples predicted accurately with probabilities ≈0.93–0.99. Marmousi: robust under velocity-model errors (train on smoothed, test on true). Versus reverse-time: MEL‑Net predicted (172, 142) with probability 0.9879 while reverse-time results varied with model error.
Latency / Detection Delay: Average positioning time ≈ 1.22×10⁻⁴ s per sample (inference).
Latency / Detection Delay Results: Same as above; substantially faster than reverse-time imaging while maintaining accuracy.
Robustness / Domain Shift: Explicit tests for velocity-model mismatch and high-noise (20%–60%); maintains high accuracy; stable imaging under complex geology.
Mode: End-to-end CNN inference after offline training.
Hardware & Compute Cost: GPU: NVIDIA GeForce RTX‑3080 Ti; Framework: PyTorch 1.6 (Python 3.8; CUDA 11.0).

Attribute/Feature Selection: None manual; features learned by CNN; multiscale feature maps (6 scales)

Class Balance: Balanced by randomly removing negative samples so positives ≈ negatives
Windowing: Sliding window: length 100, step 20

Feature Type: Learned multiscale CNN features across 6 detection scales
Feature Parameters: 6 detection scales; detectors: [MaxPool (2×1, stride 2) ×3] + [FC layers ×3 (each FC output halves input)]; kernel sizes decrease with depth
Task Framing: Binary detection/classification on windows (event vs noise); fused into sample‑level decision via C‑F model
Architecture Details: MSD‑CNN: convolution → ReLU → BatchNorm blocks across scales; each scale has its own detector; outputs fused by C‑F uncertainty reasoning (threshold 0.5)
Post-processing: Certainty Factor (C‑F) uncertainty fusion of scale‑wise detector outputs; decision if combined credibility > 0.5
Key Hyperparameters: Loss: BCE; Optimizer: SGD; Batch size: 200; Epochs: 1000; Learning rate: 0.01

Robustness / Domain Shift: Demonstrated on field data from 4 fractured wells; shows anti‑noise ability and generalization; some misdetections in high‑noise settings
Hardware & Compute Cost: Intel Core i7‑9700K CPU, 32 GB RAM; PyTorch; no GPU reported; MSD‑CNN slower than BP/CNN but faster than wavelet analysis in throughput comparisons

Attribute/Feature Selection: Learned features via GHRA (1D Ghost Convolution + Hybrid Residual Attention with channel & spatial attention); no manual feature selection
Train/Val/Test Split Protocol: Fixed split (8,986 train/val; 473 test). Fivefold CV: 80/20 train/val within folds; best‑val model evaluated on test per fold
Class Balance: Not applicable (temporal point picking rather than discrete class counts)
Windowing: Waveforms preprocessed and clipped to fixed input length (1600); dataset contains variable‑length traces aligned/windowed for model input
Denoising Ops: Core experiments use normalized data; robustness shown without explicit denoising; optional median filter improves difficult P‑picks (discussion)
Feature Type: Raw time‑series waveform samples (1D)
Feature Parameters: Key conv kernel size 5 (optimal); 4 GHRA layers in pickup network
Task Framing: Sequence labeling / arrival‑time probability estimation (point detection) for P and S
Architecture Details: GAN framework with (i) Pickup network: U‑Net‑like encoder–decoder using GHRA blocks (GhostConv + HRA), 3× max‑pool, 3× upsampling (transpose conv), 1×1 output; (ii) Discriminator: fully‑conv 1D network (GhostConv feature maps 16/32/64/128; kernel sizes 5/7/5/5) outputting a confidence curve; Application‑aware loss combining BCE‑like term with relativistic/LSGAN‑style adversarial terms
Class-Imbalance Handling: Not explicitly used (temporal regression); adversarial loss encourages alignment of predicted vs. labeled arrival distributions
Post-processing: Arrival time inferred from peaks of predicted confidence curves; qualitative examples and histograms shown; no complex post‑processing required
Key Hyperparameters: Optimizer Adam; LR 1e‑4; batch size 64; epochs 50; loss weight λ = 0.005; kernel size 5; depth 4 GHRA
Performance Metrics: Precision (P), Recall (R), F1‑score, MAE (s), inference time (ms), Params (MB)
Performance Metrics Results: Held‑out 473‑sample test (Sichuan 2011–2017): P‑wave P=0.966, R=0.941, F1=0.953, MAE=0.059 s; S‑wave P=0.934, R=0.922, F1=0.928, MAE=0.104 s. Outperforms STA/LTA, CNNDET+CGANET, UNet++ on all metrics reported
Latency / Detection Delay: Mean inference time 1.11 ms per trace (pickup), with model size 0.48 MB
Latency / Detection Delay Results: Faster than STA/LTA by ~11.59 ms; 8.5× faster than CNNDET+CGANET; ~6× faster than UNet++ (per comparative table)
Robustness / Domain Shift: Strong under noise/SNR variation down to −5.155 dB; resilient to impulse and periodic noise; validated on 2019–2020 dataset (generalization)
Mode: Supervised training offline; lightweight real‑time on‑device inference (low‑power deployment feasible)
Hardware & Compute Cost: Training/evals on RTX 3060 GPU + Ryzen 7 5800H CPU; model params 0.48 MB; inference ~1.11 ms

Attribute/Feature Selection: Raw waveform input; scaling to max amplitude per 3‑component trace; padding as needed; triangular label shaping.
Train/Val/Test Split Protocol: Pretraining on ~1M STEAD samples; validation on 20k held‑out STEAD samples; generalization test on 3710 K‑NET/KiK‑NET waveforms; TL fine‑tuned on first two DAS events from stage 27; tested on remaining 18 DAS events.
Class Balance: Not explicitly reported; diversity increased via augmentation and mixed STEAD+DAS during TL to mitigate overfitting.
Windowing: Fixed 60 s windows (typically one event per window in STEAD); augmentation can insert second event within the same window.
Normalization/Scaling: Per‑trace normalization by the maximum absolute amplitude across the three components.
Denoising Ops: DAS traces preprocessed with f–k filtering to suppress array‑direction noise and surface reflections before inference.
Feature Type: 1‑D time‑series waveforms (raw).
Feature Parameters: Sampling rate 100 Hz; triangular labels ±0.2 s around true arrival; detection window from P to S + 1.4×(S–P).
Task Framing: Multi‑task sequence labeling: simultaneous event detection and phase picking (P & S) with probabilistic outputs per time sample.
Architecture Details: EQTransformer‑inspired deep autoencoder modified with ResUNet‑style residual blocks and encoder–decoder skip connections; transformer attention removed; three decoder heads (detection/P/S).
Class-Imbalance Handling: Data augmentation and mixing of STEAD during TL; no specialized loss reweighting reported.
Post-processing: Direct use of predicted probability peaks for phase picks; qualitative comparison against SNR and DAS‑only models.
Key Hyperparameters: Not specified in the paper’s main text (e.g., learning rate/batch size not reported).
Augmentations: (1) Insert secondary earthquake within the window, (2) random temporal shifts of events, (3) gaps inserted into noise, (4) randomly drop 1–2 channels (set to zero) to simulate single‑component scenarios.
Performance Metrics: Mean Absolute Error (MAE), Standard Deviation (STD) of arrival‑time error; accuracy defined as MAE < 0.5 s for P arr.; qualitative robustness comparisons.
Performance Metrics Results: K‑NET/KiK‑NET: P‑phase MAE ≈ 0.15 s, STD ≈ 0.36 s; accuracy (MAE < 0.5 s) ≈ 97.9%. STEAD val.: augmentation reduced MAE/STD and increased ≤0.1 s and ≤0.05 s hit rates.
Robustness / Domain Shift: TL enables reliable P/S picking on noisy, single‑component DAS; outperforms SNR on high‑amplitude noise; generalizes to K‑NET/KiK‑NET despite differing characteristics.
Mode: Transfer learning of pretrained model with layer freezing (only first and last layers fine‑tuned); single‑trace DAS inference.
Hardware & Compute Cost: Not reported.

Attribute/Feature Selection: Start with 4 HCTSA features (W1–W4) from prior work; evaluate catch22 (22 features); select 4 additional features (C10, C11, C14, C15) via elastic‑net screening on validation MCC; final LR uses 8 interpretable features (no penalty).
Train/Val/Test Split Protocol: 60% / 20% / 20% by event (to prevent leakage); models trained at IR≈1.73:1; performance also tested at higher IRs via noise oversampling
Class Balance: Baseline test IR ≈ 1.73:1 (noise:event); additional tests at IR = 5:1, 10:1, 25:1, 50:1
Windowing: 30‑s windows (15 s before/after P‑pick for events; 15 s before/after random time for noise)
Denoising Ops: Band‑pass filter 5–25 Hz; downsample by ×2
Feature Type: Statistical time‑series descriptors: HCTSA (W1–W4) + catch22 (C10, C11, C14, C15)
Feature Parameters: Examples: W2 (sliding‑window stationarity over 5 windows), C11 (exp. fit of successive distances in 2‑D embedding, τ=30), plus W1/W3/W4 and C10/C14/C15 as defined in HCTSA/catch22
Task Framing: Binary classification (earthquake detection)
Architecture Details: LR (baseline): 4 HCTSA features. LR+ (augmented): 8 features (4 HCTSA + 4 catch22). CNN: GroningenNet variant retrained on event‑segregated data; input (4,3001,3); same init/optimizer/lr as benchmark; leverages multi‑level moveout.
Class-Imbalance Handling: Train at low IR as in benchmarks; evaluate robustness by progressively increasing IR in test sets; no class‑reweighting reported; early stopping and validation used.
Post-processing: None reported (decisions directly from model outputs)
Key Hyperparameters: LR/LR+: final model without elastic‑net penalty (elastic‑net only used for feature screening). CNN: benchmark settings reused (weights init, optimizer, learning rate) — specifics not enumerated in text.
Performance Metrics: Accuracy; Matthews Correlation Coefficient (MCC) (primary); confusion matrices; FN/FP counts
Performance Metrics Results: Baseline IR≈1.73:1 (test): LR 98.99% / 0.9786 MCC; LR+ 99.76% / 0.9948 MCC; CNN 99.61% / 0.9917 MCC. LR and LR+ show zero false negatives at this IR.
Robustness / Domain Shift: Tested under higher IRs: at 5:1, 10:1, 25:1, 50:1, CNN degrades less than LR, but LR and LR+ maintain zero FNs; LR+ gains grow with IR (e.g., MCC improvement over LR ≈ +36% at IR 50:1).
Mode: Offline supervised training and evaluation on curated windows
Hardware & Compute Cost: Parameter counts: CNN ≈ 283,700 parameters vs LR+ 9 weights; hardware/runtime not specified; LR models are lightweight; CNN comparatively heavier.

Attribute/Feature Selection: Time–frequency energy above noise (CATS); FK power ratio; apparent velocity (<5 km/s) filters; S‑wave semblance across channels; cross‑correlation coefficients; back‑azimuth from 3‑C geophones
Train/Val/Test Split Protocol: Not applicable

Class-Imbalance Handling: Not applicable; false‑positive control via PhaseNet and manual QC

Attribute/Feature Selection: Waveform phase picking; P/S amplitude ratios; Ant Tracking fracture attribute from 3D seismic

Normalization/Scaling: High‑pass filter at 1 Hz before picking; use of local 1‑D velocity model (VSP‑based) for association/location

Feature Type: Raw 3‑component seismic waveforms; derived seismic attributes (Ant Tracking); injection pressure/rate time series
Feature Parameters: Sampling rate 500 Hz; ≥20 P arrivals for focal mechanisms; magnitude computed via Hutton–Boore ML; local 1‑D velocity model; REAL association; VELEST absolute + hypoDD relative relocation
Task Framing: Detection → Phase picking → Association/location → Magnitude estimation → Focal mechanism inversion → Spatiotemporal/statistical analyses (b‑value, RS) → Migration analysis
Architecture Details: LOC‑FLOW workflow integrating PhaseNet (DL picker) + REAL + VELEST + hypoDD; DiTing‑FocalFlow (DiTingMotion classifier + HASH)
Post-processing: Double‑difference relocation (hypoDD); alternative absolute location cross‑check with HYPOINVERSE; QC on focal mechanisms (RMS fault‑plane uncertainties <20°)
Key Hyperparameters: High‑pass cutoff 1 Hz; sampling 500 Hz; focal mechanism computations only for ML > 0 & ≥20 P arrivals
Performance Metrics: Catalog size; location precision improvement vs. manual catalog; focal mechanism uncertainty; statistical b‑value; RS ratio (stimulation vs. total events)
Performance Metrics Results: 61,769 P and 43,628 S picks; 950 associated events (REAL); 936 after VELEST, 923 refined with hypoDD; magnitudes ML −1.29 to 1.35; 114 reliable focal mechanisms (RMS <20°); b‑value ≈1.17; RS values mostly 60–75% with mean ≈54% (lower than global ≈86%)
Latency / Detection Delay: Response time defined per stage from injection start to EQ1–EQ5; longer response time correlates with more trailing seismicity (stage‑to‑stage correlation)
Latency / Detection Delay Results: Average cross‑correlation ≈0.53 between response time (EQ3–EQ5) and trailing event count; directional migration speed along fractures ≈0.01 m/s (clusters 1–2)
Robustness / Domain Shift: Location robustness validated using HYPOINVERSE absolute locations before hypoDD (consistent results); mechanisms consistent during & after shut‑in; results dominated by events on pre‑existing fractures
Mode: Retrospective offline analysis of field recordings (Oct 23 – Nov 1, 2021 stimulation; post‑shut‑in trailing seismicity)
Hardware & Compute Cost: Not reported

Attribute/Feature Selection: Learned CNN features with RES (Residual + CBAM) modules in encoder; CCA (with Coordinate Attention) for localization decoder; CSEA (with Squeeze-and-Excitation) for denoising decoder; Gaussian mixture model labels for source locations
Train/Val/Test Split Protocol: Training/Test = 9:1; validation not specified
Windowing: Not specified; full record length used
Normalization/Scaling: Labels normalized to [0,1]; input normalization not specified
Denoising Ops: Auxiliary decoder trained in self‑supervised/Noise2Noise style; Gaussian noise added to inputs at target SNRs
Feature Type: Convolutional feature maps with attention (CBAM, CA, SE)
Feature Parameters: Encoder begins with four 5×5 conv layers (64 filters), RES blocks; three down‑sampling stages (2×2 max‑pool); decoders use 2×2 transposed conv upsampling and attention modules; ReLU activations; Sigmoid on both outputs
Task Framing: Multi‑task learning: Main = localization (Gaussian mixture regression); Auxiliary = denoising (image restoration)
Architecture Details: One encoder + two decoders; skip connections; RES (Conv+CBAM) in encoder; Decoder‑1 uses CCA (with Coordinate Attention) for localization; Decoder‑2 uses CSEA (with SE) for denoising; final outputs: 64×128 localization map and 512×64 denoised record
Class-Imbalance Handling: Weighted MSE for main task emphasizing pixels near epicenters
Post-processing: Not specified (peak(s) on localization map correspond to source locations)

Performance Metrics: Average error distance; Accuracy; Accuracy thresholds (<10 m, <20 m, <50 m)
Performance Metrics Results: Original: avg error 0.03 m; −5 dB: 0.11 m, acc 99.0% (<10 m 99.75%); −10 dB: 0.29 m, acc 97.5%; −15 dB: 0.76 m, acc 93.5% (<50 m 100%). Compared baseline U‑Net at −15 dB: avg error 2.60 m; acc 78%
Robustness / Domain Shift: Robust across SNR levels down to −15 dB; handles two and three concurrent events in simulation; real‑data validation left to future work
Mode: Offline training; feed‑forward inference (real‑time not measured)
Hardware & Compute Cost: Trained on NVIDIA Quadro RTX 4000 (8 GB VRAM), TensorFlow‑GPU 2.7.0

Attribute/Feature Selection: No hand‑crafted features; learned features from STFT images; anchor selection via data‑driven F1 analysis
Train/Val/Test Split Protocol: Chronological split: last 30% per class as test set (fixed across runs). Remaining 70% used for 5‑fold StratifiedKFold (within each fold: 80% train, 20% val).
Class Balance: Pair sampling balanced across classes during Siamese training; anchors chosen to mitigate outliers/mislabels
Windowing: 10‑second windows (2 s pre‑onset to +8 s post), sliding analysis for continuous data
Normalization/Scaling: Not specified beyond STFT magnitude usage; frequency axis preserved (no vertical resizing)
Denoising Ops: 3rd‑order Butterworth low‑pass filter at 40 Hz
Feature Type: Short‑Time Fourier Transform (STFT) spectrograms (per axis)
Feature Parameters: Sampling 250 Hz; STFT segment length 128 samples; 70% overlap; input assembled to 65×66 per axis
Task Framing: Supervised multi‑class labelling via Siamese similarity to class anchors with decision thresholding; supports sliding‑window detection
Architecture Details: Two‑branch Siamese CNN feature extractor → distance head (Cosine/Euclidean/Manhattan; Cosine chosen) → single‑neuron sigmoid; Conv2D layers with kernels (10×10), (2×2), (4×4), (4×4); MaxPooling after early layers; Dropout 0.4 on first two conv blocks; Dense(256); ~232,704 trainable params
Class-Imbalance Handling: Balanced pair generation; anchor selection removes outliers/extremes; include all examples for micro‑quake & noise classes to capture variability
Post-processing: Similarity threshold τ ≈ 0.6 for class match; exemplar anchor per class chosen by highest in‑training F1; optional multiple anchors; sliding‑window detection maps distance over time
Key Hyperparameters: Optimizer: Adam; Learning rate: 0.0005; Dropout: 0.4; Distance metric: Cosine (selected); Threshold ≈ 0.6; Data repetition factor: 10× for augmentation
Augmentations: Dataset repetition/shuffle (5–10×; best at 10×) to expand training pairs
Performance Metrics: F1, Sensitivity (TPR), Precision; confusion counts; qualitative explainability via distance heatmaps
Performance Metrics Results: Test F1 (per class): Rockfall ≈ 0.90; Earthquake ≈ 0.97; Micro‑quake ≈ 0.88; Noise ≈ 0.84. Overall few inter‑class confusions; noise most challenging. Benchmarks: comparable to multi‑class CNN on STFT; EQTransformer underperforms on these very low‑M events.
Latency / Detection Delay: Throughput ~46.5 STFT images per ms (training system); per sliding‑window detection span: Rockfall ~16 s; Micro‑quake ~2.5 s; Earthquake ~6 s (initial arrivals predominantly)
Latency / Detection Delay Results: One training epoch (with validation, rep=10) ≈ 15 s on listed hardware; detection distance curves align with average event durations
Robustness / Domain Shift: Robust to low SNR; insensitive to time shifts but sensitive to frequency shifts (class‑specific frequency bands); cross‑fold stability high for rockfall/earthquake; greater variability for micro‑quake; identifies likely mislabels via similarity analysis
Mode: Supervised classification; offline training; online/streaming‑ready inference via sliding window
Hardware & Compute Cost: Training environment: Intel i7‑10700K + NVIDIA RTX 2080 Ti; TensorFlow 2.10.1, Python 3.10.10; ~232k params (lightweight); ~15 s per epoch with dataset repetition; inference uses pre‑computed embeddings for speed

Attribute/Feature Selection: Semblance (coherence) over hyperbolic windows across channels; squared column sums to form a 1‑D coherence time‑series
Train/Val/Test Split Protocol: Not applicable
Windowing: Sliding window length N = 20 samples; time step T = 10 samples (both synthetic and real, with calibration on real data)
Normalization/Scaling: Low‑pass then downsample; remove mean & linear trend; per‑trace max normalization; band‑pass 10–250 Hz; FK filtering to suppress coherent linear noise
Denoising Ops: Low‑pass anti‑aliasing; downsampling; detrend & de‑mean; band‑pass; frequency–wavenumber (FK) filter
Feature Type: Semblance/coherence matrices (C–T grids) and derived 1‑D coherence time‑series
Feature Parameters: Curvature C ∈ [100, 180] step 5 (real data); lateral search X fixed at deepest sensor (for FORGE geometry)
Task Framing: Online/near‑real‑time event detection from DAS (binary detection with clustering)
Architecture Details: Two‑stage algorithm: (1) semblance scan along hyperbolae; (2) clustering of coherence time‑series above a data‑driven threshold + SNR gating; optional de‑clustering
Post-processing: SNR criterion (signal 30 samples ≈ 0.5 s, noise 20 samples ≈ 0.3 s) with SNR > 4; cluster merge if gap < 2 samples; de‑clustering duplicate detections within ≤ 0.7 s
Key Hyperparameters: N=20, T=10; C range 100–180 (step 5); min samples ≥ 10 above threshold to form a cluster; max gap < 2; SNR > 4; downsample to 600 Hz; band‑pass 10–250 Hz; channel interval 1350–2384 used for 78B‑32
Performance Metrics: Detection count; false‑detection rate; overlap with reference catalogue; detectable SNR limit (synthetics)
Performance Metrics Results: 24 h FORGE data: 2187 detections (after de‑clustering), 5.6% false; Silixa: 1169 detections, 6.0% false; 1086 common events; method yields ~2× detections vs standard STA/LTA; synthetic tests detect near SNR ≈ 0.1
Latency / Detection Delay: Algorithm throughput ≈ 0.25 s compute per 1 s of data on a laptop (Intel quad‑core i7, 16 GB RAM)
Latency / Detection Delay Results: ~4× faster than real time under stated hardware; suitable for near‑real‑time deployment
Robustness / Domain Shift: Works without velocity model; handles plane‑wave (low curvature) and near‑field hyperbolae; validated on synthetics and real DAS; limited to approximately straight fibres; single‑component DAS caveat
Mode: Batch over 24 h dataset; parameters and speed enable near‑real‑time operation
Hardware & Compute Cost: CPU‑only laptop (Intel i7 quad‑core, 16 GB RAM); no GPU required

Attribute/Feature Selection: Raw 3‑component window + STFT (vertical) features; fourth‑order Butterworth band‑pass (100–1800 Hz) applied beforehand
Train/Val/Test Split Protocol: First 45 min: training or active‑learning query pool; last 15 min: test. Pre‑training up to 50 epochs (early‑stopping patience 5); active‑learning fine‑tuning 15 epochs per iteration
Class Balance: Pre‑training: balanced (equal windows with and without events). Test: unbalanced. Query pool: unbalanced
Windowing: 400 ms sliding windows (1600 samples @ 4 kHz). Training windows overlap by 1/3; testing windows do not overlap

Task Framing: Event detection / binary segmentation over window (mask) with thresholded window‑level decision for querying
Architecture Details: RockNet fusion model (time + spectrogram branches); ~92,696 trainable parameters; outputs event masks
Class-Imbalance Handling: Balanced sampling during pre‑training; active‑learning batch mixes uncertain, certain‑positive, and certain‑negative samples
Post-processing: Decision threshold T ∈ {0.5, 0.6}; window max‑prob → classify into certain negative / uncertain / certain positive; acquisition selects closest to T plus random certain samples; stopping when uncertain region empty for 3 consecutive epochs
Key Hyperparameters: Batch size 128; learning rate 1e‑3; thresholds T=0.5 (Exp.1 & 3), T=0.6 (Exp.2); pre‑train ≤50 epochs (patience 5); each AL iteration 15 epochs
Performance Metrics: F1‑score (per‑window/mask‑based)
Performance Metrics Results: Exp.1 (same well/sensor type): ↑ performance; 15.5% labeled (84.5% saving). Exp.2 (different well, same sensors): F1 0.78 → 0.86 with 18.6% labeled (81.4% saving). Exp.3 (different well & sensors): F1 0.36 → 0.68 with 17% labeled (83% saving)
Latency / Detection Delay: Not directly reported; model processes ~12.7 batches/s (batch=128) in eval mode
Latency / Detection Delay Results: Throughput ≈ 1,625.6 windows/s on Titan Xp; window length 400 ms
Robustness / Domain Shift: Demonstrated transfer via active learning across wells and instrumentation; largest gains when sensor type changes
Mode: Offline evaluation with pre‑training + iterative active learning on continuous data windows
Hardware & Compute Cost: PC with 32 GB RAM and NVIDIA Titan Xp GPU; RockNet has ~92,696 trainable parameters

Attribute/Feature Selection: 60 automatically computed features from literature (Hibert, Provost, Wenner, Feng, etc.); no wrapper/embedded selection beyond algorithmic importance.

Performance Metrics: Accuracy, Precision, Recall, F1; confusion matrices; per-class precision/recall/F1 comparisons.

Highest combined scores (avg across CV runs): XGB & LGBM F1≈0.973, Precision≈0.965, Recall≈0.982; Ensembles (RF/GB/XGB/LGBM) outperform classical models.

Attribute/Feature Selection: Cross‑correlation with a reference trace to zero‑lag events; focus on S‑wave energy (field); separate CNN blocks per data type to capture curvature/frequency moveout cues; linear tokenization before Transformer.
Train/Val/Test Split Protocol: Synthetic baselines trained on 500 event +100 noise; evaluation on 200 event segments +40 noise segments. Field case: train on 1,000 synthetic; apply to field windows.
Class Balance: Training (synthetic baseline): ~5:1 events:noise; Testing: 200 events vs 40 noise.
Windowing: 1.6 s simulated segments → cross‑correlated/segmented to 0.8 s time‑lag windows.
Normalization/Scaling: Frequency/characteristics alignment via MLReal domain adaptation; wavelet matching; standardization not explicitly specified.
Denoising Ops: Field low‑pass filtering; additive Gaussian noise during training & inference; injection of recorded field noise into synthetic surface data for realism.
Feature Type: Raw waveform segments after cross‑correlation (moveout patterns across arrays).
Feature Parameters: Reference trace at selected xref; CNN feature maps unified to h×w then flattened and linearly embedded to D‑dim tokens (h, w, D chosen per data; exact values not specified).
Task Framing: Joint detection + 3D localization from simultaneous multi‑array inputs.
Architecture Details: DETR backbone adapted with multiple small CNN blocks (one per sensor type), concatenation + linear tokenization, Transformer encoder–decoder, FFN heads output p(event) and (x, y, z); set‑based Hungarian loss.
Class-Imbalance Handling: Inclusion of pure‑noise segments in training; no bespoke reweighting reported.
Post-processing: None required for predictions; validation via cross‑correlation of field vs synthetic from predicted location; comparison with least‑squares migration baseline.
Key Hyperparameters: Segment length 0.8 s (post‑Xcorr); synthetic peak freq 15–25 Hz; S‑wave focus for field; embedding/token dims not reported; training ≈12 h on a single GPU; inference ≈7 ms per window.
Augmentations: Gaussian noise (train & inference); MLReal domain adaptation (cross‑/auto‑correlation based) to bridge synthetic↔field; field‑noise injection to synthetic surface data in experiments.
Performance Metrics: Detection accuracy; location error in meters (X, Y, Z and 3D “omni”); qualitative cross‑correlation flatness; migration imaging sanity check.
Performance Metrics Results: Synthetic tests (Table 1): all cases 100% detection accuracy. Best (SF+BH via CNN blocks): mean error X=9.2 m, Y=9.8 m, Z=13.7 m, Omni=21.9 m; surface‑only Omni=24.1 m; borehole‑only Omni=41.4 m; naive concatenation Omni=52.8 m; interpolate/extrapolate Omni=104.5 m. Field case: predicted source near injection zone; cross‑correlation shows flattened moveouts; ±100 m perturbations degrade alignment; migration on noisy field data fails while synthetic migration succeeds.
Latency / Detection Delay: Model inference per window ~7 ms (trained model).
Robustness / Domain Shift: MLReal improves synthetic↔field similarity; validation with second field event and ±100 m perturbations; method leverages joint arrays to mitigate SNR and depth/azimuth ambiguities.
Mode: Offline training; near real‑time inference on streaming passive data feasible.
Hardware & Compute Cost: Training ~12 hours on a single GPU (model not specified); inference per sample ~7 ms; no special CPU costs reported.

Normalization/Scaling: Synthetic waveforms normalized prior to generation; standard DL normalization layers (AdaLIN in generator, BN/LN implied in encoder blocks)
Denoising Ops: None explicit pre‑processing; robustness achieved via deep encoder (AOG blocks) and training on noisy/generated data
Feature Type: Time‑domain 1‑D convolutional features; multi‑scale pyramid pooling in decoder
Feature Parameters: AdaLIN (γ, β, ρ) in generator; encoder depth 98 layers (ResNet‑50 base + 2× AOGBlocks; first conv split into three 3‑kernels); decoder with three ×2 upsampling layers
Task Framing: Point‑wise binary classification with softmax; pick = argmax of first‑arrival probability curve
Architecture Details: G‑LA‑MSG: U‑GAT‑IT–inspired 1‑D GAN with single generator + mapping MLP (8‑layer), identity‑feature loss, CAM loss on discriminator; AOG‑PSPNet: PSPNet encoder modified with AOGBlocks, 1‑D kernels, deeper encoder; decoder uses pyramid pooling and 3× upsample×2
Class-Imbalance Handling: Not specified (label smoothing 0.001 used during training)
Post-processing: Max‑probability time sample taken as the pick (no extra smoothing stated)
Key Hyperparameters: G‑LA‑MSG: λ₁=1, λ₂=10, λ₃=10; 10,000 iters; batch size 10. AOG‑PSPNet: Adam, lr=0.001; batch size=10; epochs=100; label smoothing=0.001.
Augmentations: Data expansion via GAN: +10,000 generated microseismic signals with varied distributions and SNRs
Performance Metrics: Accuracy on validation set (pick within 0.005 s of ground truth counted correct); timing error (s) on case studies
Performance Metrics Results: Using enhanced dataset improved validation accuracy by ~7%. AOG‑PSPNet achieved highest validation accuracy vs PSPNet_R50/R101, PhaseNet, U‑Net++ on synthetic & real tests. Reported pick errors: ≤0.005–0.006 s on synthetic (incl. −10/−15 dB and real‑noise), ≤0.002 s on real field data.
Latency / Detection Delay: Not explicitly quantified; authors note deeper model increases parameters and reduces picking speed relative to shallow networks
Robustness / Domain Shift: Evaluated across synthetic (clean + Gaussian noise at −1/−5/−10/−15 dB), synthetic + real noise, and real field datasets from Sichuan district and Shengli oilfield; maintained low timing error
Mode: Offline supervised training; feed‑forward inference for picking
Hardware & Compute Cost: CentOS 7 server; 4× NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3090 Ti (32 GB RAM total specified); PyTorch implementation

Attribute/Feature Selection: No hand‑crafted features; features learned from STFT; cosine similarity chosen for embeddings
Train/Val/Test Split Protocol: Chronological split: last 30% per class as test; 10% per class held‑out for validation; remaining for training; 5‑fold (StratifiedKFold) model selection
Class Balance: Balanced during training via augmentation (time‑shift within window) so start‑pool counts per class are equal
Windowing: 10 s windows starting 2 s before catalogue start time (sampling 250 Hz)
Denoising Ops: 3rd‑order Butterworth low‑pass at 40 Hz
Feature Type: Short‑Time Fourier Transform (STFT) per channel
Feature Parameters: Segment length 128 samples; 70% overlap (89 samples)
Task Framing: Semi‑supervised similarity learning with exemplar anchors; soft‑label distance + fixed decision threshold
Architecture Details: Siamese CNN with two identical branches → distance head (cosine) → 1‑node sigmoid; conv kernels (10×10, 2×2, 4×4, 4×4), ReLU activations, dropout 0.4 after first two conv layers, max‑pooling; dense 256; ~232,704 trainable params
Class-Imbalance Handling: Time‑shift augmentation; balanced pairing in training
Post-processing: Cosine distance score compared to decision threshold (optimal ≈ 0.6); anchor selection data‑driven by per‑class F1; soft labels used to inspect misclassifications
Key Hyperparameters: Optimizer ADAM; learning rate 0.0005; dropout 0.4; decision threshold 0.6; 10 s window; STFT params above
Augmentations: Time shifting of event start within window; rolling experiments for analysis (time/frequency)
Performance Metrics: F1‑score, sensitivity (TPR), precision; confusion‑style counts vs anchors
Performance Metrics Results: Test F1 (per class): Rockfall 0.90; Earthquake 0.97; Micro‑quake 0.88; Noise 0.84; strong agreement with manual labels and prior CNN baselines
Latency / Detection Delay: 10 s sliding window with 2 s pre‑roll; moving‑window analysis shows detection windows roughly: Rockfall ≈16 s; Earthquake ≈6 s; Micro‑quake ≈2.5 s (consistent with event durations)
Latency / Detection Delay Results: Throughput during training ≈ 46.5 STFT images/ms; with dataset repetition 10, one epoch (with validation) ≈ 15 s on stated hardware
Robustness / Domain Shift: Learned frequency‑domain features robust to time shifts; heat‑map tests show sensitivity to frequency roll (as expected); results align with prior multi‑domain CNNs; method transferable to other sites in related work
Mode: Batch/stream‑like processing on continuous recordings via sliding windows; offline evaluation with potential for near‑real‑time
Hardware & Compute Cost: Training done on Intel i7‑10700K + NVIDIA RTX 2080 Ti; TensorFlow 2.10.1; Python 3.10.10

Attribute/Feature Selection: 16 descriptors: zero-crossing rate, short-time energy, spectral centroid, roll-off, bandwidth, MFCC₂–₁₂ (11 dims)
Train/Val/Test Split Protocol: Stratified split; no samples from the same blasting event appear in multiple splits; identical splits used across models for fair comparison
Windowing: 16 partially overlapping frames; frame length 380 samples (~38 ms); hop 80; Hamming window
Normalization/Scaling: Min–max normalization to [0,1] after feature construction (primary); Z-score used in FORGE cross-domain prep
Denoising Ops: 4th-order Butterworth band-pass filter (100 Hz–4 kHz)
Feature Type: Time–frequency descriptors from 512-pt FFT; 16 mel bands (200 Hz–4 kHz); log-energies; MFCCs
Feature Parameters: 512-point FFT; 16 mel bands; MFCC coefficients 2–12; positional encoding for Transformer tokens
Task Framing: Multiclass classification (3-way)
Architecture Details: Three-branch MBHCT: (i) 1D depthwise separable conv stack (32→64, k=3), (ii) 2D conv stack (3×3, 32→64), (iii) 4-layer Transformer encoder (d=128, 4 heads, dropout≈0.1); learnable gating to fuse branches; FC 256→64; softmax; ≈4.2M params
Class-Imbalance Handling: Not required (dataset balanced); no special reweighting reported
Post-processing: None reported (direct softmax outputs)
Key Hyperparameters: Optimizer: AdamW; LR 5×10⁻⁵ (grid-searched); weight decay 0.01; dropout ≈0.2; batch size 8–32; epochs up to 200 with early stopping (patience≈15)
Performance Metrics: Accuracy, Precision, Recall, F1-score (macro and per-class), ROC-AUC (macro); statistical tests (McNemar)
Performance Metrics Results: Dongguashan test: Accuracy 98.2%, F1 98.2%, ROC-AUC 0.998; class-wise F1 ≥ 98.2%. Tongkuangyu: Accuracy ≈98.6% with top precision/recall among baselines.
Latency / Detection Delay: ~0.93 ms per 1-s trace on NVIDIA T4 (batch=1); ~<1 GB VRAM footprint; suitable for real-time edge inference
Latency / Detection Delay Results: Meets sub-second early-warning constraints; confusion-matrix errors <1–2 per 360-test split
Robustness / Domain Shift: Cross-domain Utah FORGE test: 93.4% accuracy (−4.8 pp vs. primary), highest among baselines; maintained macro-ROC ≈0.977 after 5-epoch fine-tune
Hardware & Compute Cost: ~4.2M parameters; <1 GB VRAM; profiled on single NVIDIA T4 GPU (batch=1). CPU-only performance not yet verified.

Attribute/Feature Selection: Focus on P‑wave moveout features (Z‑component). Amplitudes normalized trace‑wise; MLReal transforms used to match field/source signatures and noise.
Train/Val/Test Split Protocol: Synthetic set randomly divided 12k/3k (train/val). Inference on processed field events.
Windowing: Fixed 4‑s windows centered on detected event per Decatur preprocessing; additional 300 ms per‑trace window used in time‑domain noise purge.
Normalization/Scaling: Trace‑wise amplitude normalization to [−1, 1] within each 4‑s window.
Denoising Ops: (1) Low‑pass filter < 50 Hz to suppress 60 Hz noise; (2) notch band‑pass filters around ~20 Hz and ~30 Hz; (3) F–k dipping filter to remove incoherent energy; (4) time‑domain anomalous noise purge (300 ms window); (5) F–k envelope‑based filter derived from synthetic spectra.
Feature Type: Multichannel waveform images (trace × time) after MLReal transformation; target is 3‑D heat‑map of source likelihood.
Feature Parameters: Synthetic source: Ricker wavelet 30 Hz peak; training depth range 1.7–2.4 km; receivers per IBDP geometry; acoustic, constant‑density FD.
Task Framing: Supervised regression (event relocation via heat‑map) + unsupervised clustering (K‑means) + geometric fault‑plane fitting (least‑squares / RANSAC).
Architecture Details: Encoder‑decoder CNN producing 3‑D heat‑maps [158×198×55]; Gaussian target heat‑maps; L² loss; Adam optimizer; heat‑map spline upscaling; peak/sub‑peak interpolation to compute (x,y,z).
Post-processing: K‑means clustering by space/time (≥ baseline of 5 clusters when event count allows) → least‑squares plane fit; comparison runs with iterative elimination (90th‑percentile distance) and RANSAC to robustify plane estimation; removal of out‑of‑region/noisy events.
Key Hyperparameters: Dropout p = 0.2 after each conv layer (except last) for UQ; 50 dropout inference passes; filters as above; K‑means k based on event count (min 5). Learning rate/epochs not specified.
Augmentations: MLReal domain‑adaptation transforms: per‑section cross‑correlation to zero‑lag, convolution with (auto)correlations to inject field source/noise/reflectivity characteristics into synthetic data; reciprocal transform at inference.
Performance Metrics: Agreement with relocated catalog (hypocentral distance); qualitative fault plane alignment; uncertainty ellipsoids (std‑dev from dropout); computational latency.
Performance Metrics Results: Median / mean hypocentral differences vs catalog: 0.04229 km / 0.06228 km (good agreement). Uncertainty: ~≤ 0.15 km for isolated clusters; up to ~0.8 km in dense clusters near injection. Fault strikes align NE–SW with prior studies.
Latency / Detection Delay: ~4 s per seismic event end‑to‑end inference (signal enhancement + relocation + fault fitting) on a single NVIDIA V100 GPU.
Robustness / Domain Shift: MLReal reduces synthetic‑to‑field covariate shift; results consistent across K‑means/iterative/RANSAC plane fits; short‑term (30 d) and long‑term (1,440 d) windows show similar spatial patterns; dropout‑based UQ quantifies reliability.
Mode: Passive seismic monitoring; 3‑D relocation and fault delineation; field inference with synthetic‑trained model.
Hardware & Compute Cost: Inference: single V100 GPU, ~4 s/event. Training: dual A100 GPUs, ~12 h. Synthetic generation: ~30 min/sample on CPU (one‑time; 15k samples).

Attribute/Feature Selection: Encoder outputs 16 feature maps → squared features → standard‑deviation screening selects top 8 scales/features for clustering.
Train/Val/Test Split Protocol: Not applicable / not reported (unsupervised CAE + clustering; evaluation versus noise‑free baseline).
Windowing: CWT (Morlet) to convert traces to time–frequency domain; conv stride performs downsampling along scale.
Denoising Ops: CWT for frequency‑domain separation; CAE feature learning/reconstruction; standard‑deviation feature screening prior to K‑means.
Feature Type: CAE latent feature maps derived from time–frequency images (16 channels → top‑8 by std‑dev).
Feature Parameters: Morlet CWT; conv kernels 3×3; encoder filters per layer [2, 4, 8, 16]; conv stride 1×4 (scale downsampling); decoder transposed conv stride 1×4; sigmoid output layer.
Task Framing: (A) Unsupervised waveform picking/denoising; (B) Physics‑based reverse‑time migration imaging for source localization.
Architecture Details: Encoder: 4 blocks [Conv → BN → ReLU → Dropout]. Decoder: 5 layers mirroring encoder + final conv (sigmoid).
Post-processing: Multiply 0/1 labels with original traces to extract waveforms; reverse‑time localization with maximum‑amplitude and cross‑correlation imaging; PML absorbing boundaries; geophone grouping (n=5) for cross‑correlation to reduce compute.
Key Hyperparameters: k‑means k=2; encoder filters [2,4,8,16]; kernel 3×3; strides 1×4 (enc/dec scale axis); imaging uses 5 geophone groups; PML damping in x/z (values per region as described).
Performance Metrics: Picking Accuracy (Acc) versus noise‑free baseline; qualitative localization fidelity; SNR scenarios.
Performance Metrics Results: Acc >96% for SNR in [−11.40 dB, −5.33 dB]; at −8.28 dB: picking 98.57% Acc and localization nearly identical to noise‑free images; at −14.89 dB: noticeable mispicks due to noise dominance.
Robustness / Domain Shift: Robust across multiple SNRs; validated on real coal‑mine channel‑wave records (effective extraction and accurate source imaging).
Hardware & Compute Cost: Not reported.

Attribute/Feature Selection: NMF on spectrograms to obtain W (dictionary) and H (activations); H modeled by HMM; state‑transition frequency matrices (“fingerprints”) used for clustering; PCA used for visualization.
Train/Val/Test Split Protocol: Not applicable (unsupervised; analysis on full event sets for each MS cluster).
Class Balance: Not enforced; Cluster 0 associated with higher magnitudes than Cluster 1 within bursts.
Windowing: STFT window 144 samples, 132‑sample overlap; burst windows defined by daily event‑rate thresholds.
Normalization/Scaling: Not explicitly specified beyond bandpass filtering; standardization not reported.
Denoising Ops: Bandpass filter 10–400 Hz prior to feature extraction.
Feature Type: Time‑dependent spectral fingerprints (HMM state transition frequencies) derived from NMF activations.
Feature Parameters: NMF rank r = 30 (elbow in reconstruction error); HMM with 15 hidden states, exponential emissions; K‑means defaults (k‑means++ init, 300 max iters, tol 1e‑4).
Task Framing: Unsupervised discovery of event patterns + physical interpretation via bursts and focal mechanisms.
Architecture Details: NMF (NIMFA); HMM (Pomegranate); K‑means & PCA (scikit‑learn); HASH solver for focal mechanisms; polarity + S/P amplitude ratio measurements from multiple borehole stations.
Class-Imbalance Handling: None reported (unsupervised); interpretation includes magnitude statistics per cluster.
Post-processing: Burst detection (daily rate threshold α: 0.5 for MS#2; 1.5 for MS#4; min duration 2 days; de‑triggering rule); focal mechanisms filtered to fault‑plane uncertainty ≤ 35°.
Key Hyperparameters: Bandpass 10–400 Hz; STFT window 144 / overlap 132; NMF rank 30; HMM states 15; KMeans K = 2; burst α = 0.5 (MS#2) & 1.5 (MS#4); min burst = 2 days; HASH grid 5° strike/dip/rake; weights: polarity 1.0, S/P 0.1–0.2.
Performance Metrics: Silhouette score; PCA separability; NMF reconstruction‑error elbow; statistical tests (t‑test, Mann–Whitney U, Bartlett’s test) on magnitudes/fingerprint variance; focal‑mechanism uncertainty statistics across station configs.
Performance Metrics Results: Silhouette (MS#2): K=2 (0.0399), K=3 (0.0422); Silhouette (MS#4): K=2 (0.0695). Cluster 0 shows significantly higher magnitudes (p<0.05) in both MS#2 and MS#4; Bartlett’s F=754.166, p<1e‑5 indicating higher variance in Cluster 1 fingerprints. Multi‑station + S/P reduces mean fault‑plane uncertainty from ~60.4° (single) → ~31.5°.
Latency / Detection Delay: Not reported (offline analysis; supplementary notes mention processing times, not detailed in main text).
Robustness / Domain Shift: Uncertainty analysis on focal mechanisms vs. station configurations; discussion of network geometry and velocity‑model sensitivity; notes on stochastic initialization for HMM/K‑means and potential variability.
Mode: Offline post‑hoc analysis of recorded microseismicity (first year focus).
Hardware & Compute Cost: Not reported.

Attribute/Feature Selection: End‑to‑end learned features; dynamic snake convolution (horizontal & vertical) to capture narrow, elongated event morphology; no manual feature selection
Train/Val/Test Split Protocol: 9 : 1 : 1 split of 7,480 slices
Class Balance: 6,480 event slices vs 1,000 pure‑noise slices (imbalance addressed via augmentation and weighted loss)
Windowing: Continuous data sliced into 2D patches; additional downsampling to multiple sampling rates (250–2000 Hz)
Denoising Ops: Median and band‑pass filtering pre‑processing; denoising augmentation
Feature Type: Raw DAS time–channel slices (image‑like tensors); CAMs used for interpretability
Feature Parameters: Sampling rates 250–2000 Hz; noise level β ∈ [0,1]; area threshold Thre_area = 10,000; WIoU λ = 0.7; confidence threshold 0.5
Task Framing: Object detection reframed as semantic segmentation + connected‑domain (two‑stage) algorithm to produce boxes & confidence
Architecture Details: Dynamic Snake Conv module → MobileNetV3 backbone → LRASPP head with channel‑attention; bilinear upsampling; output thresholded at 0.5; segmentation‑error correction (weighted matrix + extra penalty)
Class-Imbalance Handling: Data augmentations; segmentation weight matrix emphasizing far‑from‑event background; extra penalty for misclassified pixels (α = 0.1)
Post-processing: Two‑stage connected‑domain algorithm; small‑area filtering using Thre_area; confidence = sum(Out)/sum(Seg_out) over each box
Key Hyperparameters: 50 epochs; batch size 16; Adam (momentum 0.9); initial LR 0.001 with cosine annealing; TP criteria: WIoU ≥ 0.5 and Score ≥ 0.5; α = 0.1; λ = 0.7; Thre_area = 10,000
Augmentations: Slicing, normalization, translation (time shift), denoising, downsampling, additive noise mixing
Performance Metrics: Precision, Recall, F1‑score; also parameter size and detection speed
Performance Metrics Results: Best F1‑score 0.951 with correction strategy; Precision ≈ 0.978, Recall ≈ 0.924; model size ≈ 13 MB; dynamic snake raises compute modestly (still lightweight)
Latency / Detection Delay: Not explicitly reported; designed and demonstrated for real‑time feasibility on terminal devices
Robustness / Domain Shift: Robust under added noise, time‑translation, and across sampling rates 250–2000 Hz; stable detection shown in visualizations
Mode: Supervised deep learning (semantic segmentation used for detection)
Hardware & Compute Cost: Training: 2× Nvidia RTX A4000 16GB, Ubuntu 18.04.6, PyTorch 1.11 (CUDA 11.4); Inference: lightweight (MobileNetV3 + LRASPP), suitable for real‑time terminal deployment

Attribute/Feature Selection: MLReal domain adaptation (cross/auto‑correlation + convolution) to time‑lag domain; segmentation around zero‑lag; optional denoising (non‑local means) and removal of linear‑moveout traces in field data
Train/Val/Test Split Protocol: Fixed numbers listed above for training; testing on 1000 samples per class (0/1/2/3 events) in SEAM example; three single‑event field segments for Arkoma example
Class Balance: Intentionally fewer zero‑event samples; classification loss down‑weighted vs. localization (1:9)
Windowing: Long recordings sliced to short segments; cross‑correlation moves events to zero‑lag; window around zero‑lag is used
Normalization/Scaling: Distribution alignment via MLReal; resampling after correlation (no explicit amplitude normalization reported)
Denoising Ops: White Gaussian noise injected during training/validation; same SNR (~5) at inference for tests; non‑local means denoising on field data before inference
Feature Type: Time‑lag domain array features (cross/auto‑correlated and convolved seismograms)
Feature Parameters: Reference trace for cross‑correlation; mean auto‑correlated synthetic stack for application MLReal; frequency band ~5–20 Hz considered in analysis
Task Framing: Multi‑task: event detection (classification) + source location (regression) with set‑prediction
Architecture Details: CNN backbone → Transformer encoder–decoder (DETR) → FFN with two heads (classification & regression); N=5 prediction slots; event probability threshold ≈ 0.7; bipartite (Hungarian) matching for set loss
Post-processing: None required for inference (end‑to‑end). Kriging/Gaussian smoothing used only for visualization of error maps in the paper
Key Hyperparameters: N (slots)=5; detection threshold ≈0.7; Hungarian loss combining BCE (cls) + MSE (loc) with 1:9 weighting
Augmentations: Multi‑event synthesis by stacking single‑event simulations; additive Gaussian noise; MLReal domain adaptation
Performance Metrics: (i) Classification accuracy / confusion matrix; (ii) Mean location error (m); (iii) Runtime per segment
Performance Metrics Results: SEAM classification (correct class %): 0‑event 100%; 1‑event 91.8%; 2‑event 93.5%; 3‑event 98.4. SEAM mean location error: 1‑event 18.4 m; 2‑event 25.6 m; 3‑event 39.8 m
Latency / Detection Delay: Inference on order of milliseconds per segment; ~0.007 s/segment in SEAM benchmark; ~6.9 ms/segment on Arkoma field segments
Latency / Detection Delay Results: 1000 segments processed in <10 s by DETR; least‑squares migration took ~150 s/segment in comparison (non‑optimized)
Robustness / Domain Shift: MLReal bridges synthetic ↔ field; noise sensitivity study: detection & localization degrade with SNR↓; resilient compared to migration at low SNR; failure around SNR=0.01
Hardware & Compute Cost: Specific hardware not reported; relative compute: DETR ≪ migration (orders of magnitude faster at inference)

Attribute/Feature Selection: Differential travel‑time relative to station with shortest arrival; uses receiver network on surface; sampling of grid points across domain
Train/Val/Test Split Protocol: For each model (layered, fault, SEAM): 1,000 training sources; 1,000 random test sources. ToC2ME: 1,000 training + 1,000 test events with added noise on training; error reported on test set
Windowing: Not applicable (first‑arrival travel‑times)
Normalization/Scaling: Physics and data losses normalized to similar scales; δ added to avoid division by zero in loss
Denoising Ops: Data augmentation with Gaussian noise up to 5% on synthetic/ToC2ME training times to simulate picking error
Feature Type: Differential travel‑time vectors; spatial coordinates; velocity field samples
Feature Parameters: Surface receiver layouts: layered (65), fault (49), SEAM (49), ToC2ME (65); grid spacings 0.05 km (e.g., 61×61×51; 101×101×101; 141×159×71)
Task Framing: Learn travel‑time field T(x,y,z)**; localize source by argmin T over grid
Architecture Details: DeepONet with branch_net (input=#stations → 64 → 128), trunk_net (3 → 64 → 128), root_net (128 → 64 → 1); replaces MLP with Fourier Analysis Network (FAN) hybrid activations (sin, cos, GELU); optional root_net enrichment
Post-processing: Choose grid point with minimum predicted travel‑time as source location
Key Hyperparameters: Loss = L_Data + L_Eikonal with small δ; training iterations: 200–500 depending on model; optimizer/framework: PyTorch autograd (specific lr/batch not stated)
Augmentations: Additive Gaussian noise (≤5%) to travel‑times during training to emulate pick errors
Performance Metrics: Mean absolute localization error (km) overall and by direction (x, y, z); qualitative overlap of true vs. predicted minima; speed
Performance Metrics Results: Layered: mean 0.029 km (x=0.011, y=0.010, z=0.011). Fault: mean 0.033 km (x=0.012, y=0.013, z=0.014) and better than RFloc3D (0.071 km). SEAM: mean 0.046 km (x=0.017, y=0.016, z=0.029). ToC2ME: mean 0.134 km (x=0.057, y=0.056, z=0.086). Most errors <0.1–0.25 km depending on case
Latency / Detection Delay Results: Claimed sub‑second localization demonstrated in numerical/field tests
Robustness / Domain Shift: Robust to sparse receivers and noisy picks; experiments across 17–53 geophones show small error changes; training source count study shows performance stabilizes ≥700 sources
Hardware & Compute Cost: Experiments on NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3090 (24 GB); example training times (fault model study): ~18–183 min across 100–1,000 sources; ToC2ME/larger models trained for ~500 iters
